summary: Recent updates to the Media sink area in Firefox have focused on improving the reliability and safety of audio handling. One significant change ensures that the audio processing system properly waits for necessary initialization tasks to finish during shutdown, which helps prevent potential audio playback issues. Additionally, the use of checked arithmetic for managing buffer lengths in audio decoding ensures more stable performance, reducing the risk of buffer overflow errors. Collectively, these improvements enhance the overall audio experience in the browser, supporting smoother and more reliable media playback.
